author: nc1234
content: I was gonna ask the same question. If PTC_SIZE is fixed, then `IndexedAttestation.attesting_indices` is progressive while `IndexedPayloadAttestation.attesting_indices` is not, so there is an assymetry. But if we are certain that PTC_SIZE never changes, then I guess it's fine

author: etan_status
content:
IndexedPayloadAttestation is a fake type anyway, I'm not sure why that one even is ssz, it's not used in any container and not used over the network.
The core inconsistency is for PayloadAttestation which uses a Bitvector, vs the regular AggregateWithProof that uses a variable length list.
But if PTC_SIZE stays constant, that's intentional. It's akin to the sync committee messages / sync contributions.
<@755590043632140352> mentioned to me that he suspects PTC_SIZE will stay as this forever, but also "I'm not sure".
Bitvector vs Bitlist is the only decision to make. If PTC_SIZE can evolve, Bitlist, otherwise, Bitvector.

author: nc1234
content: Team hasn't reviewed the code thoroughly but at least it's passing spec test and AI is happy with the code. Gonna run a local kurtosis tmr

content: probably worth keeping invariant of reference test vectors before devnet launch, whenever they do come out?
